classes.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver.resolve_distinguishedname
documentation:
Resolve a DistinguishedName in LDAP. This will use the GC by default
Returns a single LDAP entry

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver.resolve_samname
documentation:
Resolve a SAM name in the GC. This can give multiple results.
Returns a list of LDAP entries

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver.resolve_upn
documentation:
Resolve a UserPrincipalName in the GC.
Returns a single LDAP entry

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver.resolve_sid
documentation:
Resolve a SID in LDAP. This will use the GC by default
Returns a single LDAP entry

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.objectresolver.ObjectResolver.gc_sam_lookup
documentation:
This function attempts to resolve the SAM name returned in session enumeration to
a user/domain combination by querying the Global Catalog.
SharpHound calls this GC Deconflictation.

This class is responsible for resolving objects. This can be for example sAMAccountNames which
should be resolved in the GC to see which domain they belong to, or SIDs which have to be
resolved somewhere else. This resolver is thread-safe.

classes.name: bloodhound.enumeration.computers.ComputerEnumerator

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.computers.ComputerEnumerator.__init__
documentation:
Computer enumeration. Enumerates all computers in the given domain.
Every domain enumerated will get its own instance of this class.

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.computers.ComputerEnumerator.enumerate_computers
documentation:
Enumerates the computers in the domain. Is threaded, you can specify the number of workers.
Will spawn threads to resolve computers and enumerate the information.

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.computers.ComputerEnumerator.process_computer
documentation: Processes a single computer, pushes the results of the computer to the given queue.

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.computers.ComputerEnumerator.work
documentation: Work function, will obtain work from the given queue and will push results on the results_q.

Class to enumerate computers in the domain.
Contains the threading logic and workers which will call the collection
methods from the bloodhound.ad module.

This class extends the MembershipEnumerator class just to inherit the
membership lookup functions which are also needed for computers.

Contains the dumping functions which

functions.name: bloodhound.enumeration.acls.parse_binary_acl
documentation:
Main ACL structure parse function.
This is offloaded to subprocesses and takes the current entry and the
acl data as argument. This is then returned and processed back in the main process

functions.name: bloodhound.enumeration.acls.can_write_property
documentation:
Checks if the access is sufficient to write to a specific property.
This can either be because we have the right ADS_RIGHT_DS_WRITE_PROP and the correct GUID
is set in ObjectType, or if we have the ADS_RIGHT_DS_WRITE_PROP right and the ObjectType
is empty, in which case we can write to any property. This is documented in
[MS-ADTS] section 5.1.3.2: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc223511.aspx

functions.name: bloodhound.enumeration.acls.has_extended_right
documentation:
Checks if the access is sufficient to control the right with the given GUID.
This can either be because we have the right ADS_RIGHT_DS_CONTROL_ACCESS and the correct GUID
is set in ObjectType, or if we have the ADS_RIGHT_DS_CONTROL_ACCESS right and the ObjectType
is empty, in which case we have all extended rights. This is documented in
[MS-ADTS] section 5.1.3.2: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc223511.aspx

functions.name: bloodhound.enumeration.acls.ace_applies
documentation:
Checks if an ACE applies to this object (based on object classes).
Note that this function assumes you already verified that InheritedObjectType is set (via the flag).
If this is not set, the ACE applies to all object types.

classes.name: bloodhound.enumeration.acls.AclEnumerator
documentation: Helper class for ACL parsing.

documentation: Membership enumeration. Enumerates all groups/users/other memberships.

documentation:
Attempt to resolve the membership (DN) of a group to an object
Moved to addomain logic since we need DN resolving in other files

documentation: Construct primary membership from RID to SID (BloodHound 3.0 only)

documentation: Resolve properties for user objects

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.memberships.MembershipEnumerator.enumerate_computers_dconly
documentation:
Enumerate computer objects. This function is only used if no
collection was requested that required connecting to computers anyway.

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.memberships.MembershipEnumerator.parse_gmsa
documentation: Parse GMSA DACL which states which users can read the password

documentation: Process ACLs that resulted from parsing with cstruct

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.memberships.MembershipEnumerator.write_default_users

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.memberships.MembershipEnumerator.write_default_groups
documentation: Put default groups in the groups.json file

methods.name: bloodhound.enumeration.memberships.MembershipEnumerator.enumerate_memberships

Class to enumerate memberships in the domain.
Contains the dumping functions which
methods from the bloodhound.ad module.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.ldap_connect
documentation: Connect to the LDAP service

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.gc_connect
documentation: Connect to the global catalog

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.search
documentation: Search for objects in LDAP or Global Catalog LDAP.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.ldap_get_single
documentation:
Get a single object, requires full DN to object.
This function supports searching both in the local directory and the Global Catalog.
The connection to the GC should already be established before calling this function.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.get_objecttype
documentation: Function to get objecttype GUID

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.get_domains
documentation: Function to get domains. This should only return the current domain.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.get_forest_domains
documentation:
Function which searches the LDAP references in order to find domains.
I'm not sure if this is the best function but couldn't find anything better.

This searches the configuration, which is present only once in the forest but is replicated
to every DC.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.get_computers
documentation:
Get all computer objects. This purely gets them using LDAP. This function is used directly in case of DCOnly enum,
or used to create a cache in case of computer enumeration later on.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.domain.ADDC.get_computers_withcache
documentation:
Get all computer objects. This is the function used when we do computer enumeration, in which case these
items are cached first. Calls the function with generator above to create the list.

functions.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.ctypes
documentation: Create ctypes structures from cstruct structures.

functions.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.hexdump
documentation:
Hexdump some data.

Args:
    s: Bytes to hexdump.
    palette: Colorize the hexdump using this color pattern.
    offset: Byte offset of the hexdump.
    prefix: Optional prefix.

functions.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.dumpstruct
documentation:
Dump a structure or parsed structure instance.

Prints a colorized hexdump and parsed structure output.

Args:
    t: Structure or Instance to dump.
    data: Bytes to parse the Structure on, if t is not a parsed Instance.
    offset: Byte offset of the hexdump.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct.addtype
documentation:
Add a type or type reference.

Args:
    name: Name of the type to be added.
    t: The type to be added. Can be a str reference to another type
        or a compatible type class.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the type already exists.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct.load
documentation:
Parse structures from the given definitions using the given definition type.

Definitions can be parsed using different parsers. Currently, there's
only one supported parser - DEF_CSTYLE. Parsers can add types and
modify this cstruct instance. Arguments can be passed to parsers
using kwargs.

Args:
    s: The definition to parse.
    deftype: The definition type to parse the definitions with.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments for parsers.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct.loadfile
documentation:
Load structure definitions from a file.

The given path will be read and parsed using the .load() function.

Args:
    s: The path to load definitions from.
    deftype: The definition type to parse the definitions with.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments for parsers.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct.read
documentation:
Parse data using a given type.

Args:
    name: Type name to read.
    s: File-like object or byte string to parse.

Returns:
    The parsed data.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.cstruct.resolve
documentation:
Resolve a type name to get the actual type object.

Types can be referenced using different names. When we want
the actual type object, we need to resolve these references.

Args:
    name: Type name to resolve.

Returns:
    The resolved type object.

Raises:
    ResolveError: If the type can't be resolved.

Main class of cstruct. All types are registered in here.

Args:
    endian: The endianness to use when parsing.
    pointer: The pointer type to use for Pointers.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Parser

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Parser.parse
documentation:
This function should parse definitions to cstruct types.

Args:
    data: Data to parse definitions from, usually a string.

Base class for definition parsers.

Args:
    cstruct: An instance of cstruct.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.CStyleParser

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.CStyleParser.parse
documentation: TODO: Implement proper parsing

Definition parser for C-like structure syntax.

Args:
    cstruct: An instance of cstruct
    compiled: Whether structs should be compiled or not.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Instance

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Instance.write
documentation:
Write this structure to a writable file-like object.

Args:
    fh: File-like objects that supports writing.

Returns:
    The amount of bytes written.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Instance.dumps
documentation:
Dump this structure to a byte string.

Returns:
    The raw bytes of this structure.
Holds parsed structure data.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.PointerInstance
documentation: Like the Instance class, but for structures referenced by a pointer.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Expression
documentation: Expression parser for simple calculations in definitions.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.reads
documentation:
Parse the given data according to the type that implements this class.

Args:
    data: Byte string to parse.

Returns:
    The parsed value of this type.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.dumps
documentation:
Dump the given data according to the type that implements this class.

Args:
    data: Data to dump.

Returns:
    The resulting bytes.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.read
documentation:
Parse the given data according to the type that implements this class.

Args:
    obj: Data to parse. Can be a (byte) string or a file-like object.

Returns:
    The parsed value of this type.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.write
documentation:
Write the given data to a writable file-like object according to the
type that implements this class.

Args:
    stream: Writable file-like object to write to.
    data: Data to write.

Returns:
    The amount of bytes written.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.default
documentation: Return a default value of this type.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BaseType.default_array
documentation: Return a default array of this type.
Base class for cstruct type classes.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.RawType
documentation: Base class for raw types that have a name and size.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Structure

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Structure.add_field
documentation:
Add a field to this structure.

Args:
    name: The field name.
    type_: The field type.
    offset: The field offset.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Structure.default
documentation:
Create and return an empty Instance from this structure.

Returns:
    An empty Instance from this structure.

methods.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Structure.show
documentation: Pretty print this structure.
Type class for structures.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BitBuffer
documentation: Implements a bit buffer that can read and write bit fields.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Field
documentation: Holds a structure field.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Array
documentation:
Implements a fixed or dynamically sized array type.

Example:
    When using the default C-style parser, the following syntax is supported:

        x[3] -> 3 -> static length.
        x[] -> None -> null-terminated.
        x[expr] -> expr -> dynamic length.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.PackedType
documentation: Implements a packed type that uses Python struct packing characters.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.CharType
documentation: Implements a character type that can properly handle strings.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.WcharType
documentation: Implements a wide-character type.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.BytesInteger
documentation: Implements an integer type that can span an arbitrary amount of bytes.

classes.name: bloodhound.lib.cstruct.Enum

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.rpc_get_hostname
documentation:
Authenticated hostname resolution. Requires admin rights.
Currently not in use - does not respect authentication stack

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.get_ntlm_hostname
documentation: Unauthenticated hostname resolution with NTLM challenge/response

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.formatSid
documentation: Translate the binary SID from LDAP into human-readable form

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.translateSidType
documentation: Translate SidType to strings accepted by BloodHound

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.is_dc
documentation: Check whether the computer object is a Domain Controller

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.resolve_ad_entry
documentation:
Translate an LDAP entry into a dictionary containing the
information used by BloodHound

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.resolve_sid_entry
documentation:
Convert LsarLookupSids entries to entries for the SID cache, which should match
the format from the resolve_ad_entry function.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.get_entry_property
documentation:
Simple wrapper that gets an attribute from ldap3 dictionary,
converting empty values to the default specified. This is primarily
for output to JSON

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.win_timestamp_to_unix
documentation:
Convert Windows timestamp (100 ns since 1 Jan 1601) to
unix timestamp.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.parse_task_xml
documentation:
Parse scheduled task XML and extract the user and logon type with
regex. Is not a good way to parse XMLs but saves us the whole parsing
overhead.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.ensure_string
documentation:
Sometimes properties can contain binary data. Since we can't assume encoding, make
sure it can be outputted as json

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.ADUtils.parse_gplink_string
documentation:
Parse GP Link string according to MS-GPOL
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/windows_protocols/ms-gpol/08090b22-bc16-49f4-8e10-f27a8fb16d18

classes.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.AceResolver
documentation:
This class resolves ACEs containing rights, acetype and a SID to Aces containing
BloodHound principals, which can be outputted to json.
This is mostly a wrapper around the sid resolver calls

classes.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.DNSCache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.DNSCache.get
documentation: Get an entry from the cache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.DNSCache.put
documentation: Put a forward lookup in the cache, this also
puts the reverse lookup in the cache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.DNSCache.put_single
documentation: Put a reverse lookup in the cache. Forward lookup
is not added since reverse is considered less reliable

A cache used for caching forward and backward DNS at the same time.
This cache is used to avoid PTR queries when forward lookups are already done

classes.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.SidCache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.SidCache.get
documentation: Get an entry from the cache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.SidCache.put
documentation: Put a forward lookup in the cache, this also
puts the reverse lookup in the cache

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.SidCache.load
documentation: Overwrite cache from disk
Generic cache for caching SID lookups

classes.name: bloodhound.ad.utils.SamCache
documentation:
Cache for mapping SAM names to principals.
Identical to the SidCache in behaviour

modules.name: bloodhound.__main__
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: bloodhound.ad.dumpntlm
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Impacket - Collection of Python classes for working with network protocols.
SECUREAUTH LABS. Copyright (C) 2022 SecureAuth Corporation. All rights reserved.
This software is provided under a slightly modified version
of the Apache Software License. See the accompanying LICENSE file
for more information.
Description:
Dump remote host information in ntlm authentication model, without credentials.
For SMB protocols (1 and 2/3), it's easy to use SMBConnection class (thanks to @agsolino),
but since negotiate response is not available in original classes,
we made out custom classes based on them.
The usefull information in negotiate response are "Dialect Version", "Signing Options",
"Maximum bytes allowed per smb request" and "Servers time information".
The point is sometimes server dosn't include "boot time" in response. But we show it,
when available, in this script.
It's very easy to use:
python dumpntlm.py 192.168.1.63
Author:
Alex Romero (@NtAlexio2)
Reference for:
[MS-SMB2]
[MS-RPCE]
NOTE:
This is modified version of original dumpntlm.py here:
https://github.com/NtAlexio2/impacket/blob/master/examples/dumpntlm.py
The relevant pull request (https://github.com/SecureAuthCorp/impacket/pull/1404)
to Impacket is not accepted at the time of adding this file to BloodHound.py repo.

documentation: Request a Kerberos TGT given our provided inputs.

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.authentication.ADAuthentication.get_tgs_for_smb
documentation:
Get a TGS for use with SMB Connection. We do this here to make sure the realms are correct,
since impacket doesn't support cross-realm TGT usage and we don't want it to do its own Kerberos

classes.name: bloodhound.ad.computer.ADComputer

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.computer.ADComputer.rpc_get_loggedon
documentation:
Query logged on users via RPC.
Requires admin privs

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.computer.ADComputer.rpc_get_services
documentation:
Query services with stored credentials via RPC.
These credentials can be dumped with mimikatz via lsadump::secrets or via secretsdump.py

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.computer.ADComputer.rpc_get_schtasks
documentation:
Query the scheduled tasks via RPC. Requires admin privileges.
These credentials can be dumped with mimikatz via vault::cred

methods.name: bloodhound.ad.computer.ADComputer.rpc_resolve_sids
documentation: Resolve any remaining unknown SIDs for local accounts.
Computer connected to Active Directory
